summary: General Motors temporarily laid off around 1,300 workers at Factory Zero after idling EV production, then installed about 50 collaborative robot arms at the plant as part of a broader push to bring advanced automation into manufacturing operations.
evidenceQuote: General Motors has temporarily laid off around 1,300 workers at its Factory Zero electric vehicle plant in Michigan for a 30-day period as the automaker adjusts production to meet market demand for EVs.
notes: WardsAuto reported that GM spokesperson Kevin Kelly confirmed the approximately 1,300-worker temporary layoff, effective March 16 through April 13, citing Reuters. Ars Technica later reported that 1,300 workers remained out of work while GM installed approximately 50 FANUC robot arms at Factory Zero. The New York Post rounded the affected workforce as more than 1,000. This is separate from GM's May 2026 IT department layoffs and from a prior Factory Zero permanent layoff of about 1,200 workers in late 2025.

sources.name: WardsAuto
sources.quote: General Motors has temporarily laid off around 1,300 workers at its Factory Zero electric vehicle plant in Michigan for a 30-day period as the automaker adjusts production to meet market demand for EVs.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.quote: Dozens of new robot arms have been installed at General Motors' flagship electric vehicle factory in Detroit, even as 1,300 workers remain out of work following what was supposed to be a temporary layoff.

sources.name: New York Post
sources.quote: General Motors has gutted its electric-vehicle ambitions and sidelined more than 1,000 jobs at its flagship Detroit assembly plant - while adding 50 robots, sparking outrage from labor unions.
